name: Fidha
title: Loyal Servant
shortDescription: The loyal maid of Hazrat Fatima Zahra (SA) who accompanied the Ahl al-Bayt to Karbala and stood by them during captivity.
fullStory:
Fidha al-Nubiyya was the loyal and pious maid of Hazrat Fatima Zahra (SA). She was a devout woman, knowledgeable in the Quran, and highly respected by the Prophet's (PBUH) household. It is said that for years, Fidha spoke only using verses from the Quran.

Her devotion to the Prophet's family was so deep that after the martyrdom of Hazrat Zahra (SA), she remained with Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and his children. When Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan set out for Karbala, Fidha, despite her old age, accompanied Lady Zaynab (SA) and the other women on this perilous journey.

On Ashura, with tearful eyes, Fidha witnessed the martyrdom of Fatima's (SA) children. She nursed the children in the tents and consoled the women.

After the tents were looted, one of her most famous actions occurred when barebacked camels were brought for the captives. Out of immense grief and to protect the Ahl al-Bayt's sanctity, she sought the help of a lion in the vicinity to prevent further disrespect by Yazid's soldiers (a famous scene in Ta'ziyeh plays). During the captivity, Fidha patiently remained a confidante and companion to Lady Zaynab (SA).
quoteArabic: وَكانَتْ فِضَّةُ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْها امْرَأَةً صالِحَةً...
quote: (Describing her:) Fidha was a righteous woman who spoke only with the Quran.
